_doc: Phase 2 scenario-stress mechanism scores. For each scenario, each calibrated indicator's peak-stress value is percentiled against the indicator's full live sample (post-2011) and direction-corrected 0-100 (higher = more stress) using the same convention as the live v11 cycle-mechanism scorer. Mechanism score = simple-average of available indicator scores; composite = simple-average of mechanism scores. Indicators with value=null (e.g. RRP pre-2013) or no historical sample are dropped from the denominator and listed in indicators_dropped per scenario. Refreshed nightly by scripts/compute_scenario_mechanism_stress.py at 23:00 UTC weekdays.

calibration_decisions.context: 5 methodology decisions taken at Phase 2B kickoff before scenario calibration begins. All five locked via AskUserQuestion popup; Senior Quant + Lead Dev consulted, UX Designer and Data Steward will sign off at Phase 2H.

q1_pre_1996_proxies.rule: Use named substitutes for indicators that didn't exist before 1996.
specifics: HY spread (ICE BofA BAMLH0A0HYM2, 1996): pre-1996 use Moody's BAA-Treasury corporate spread.
Tag every substitution in source_note as 'proxy: <substitute name>'.

q2_pre_2014_gdpnow.rule: For pre-2014 scenarios, use the BEA advance-GDP release for the quarter aligned with the scenario peak in place of GDPNow.
specifics: Footnote: 'real-time forecast unavailable; realized print used'.
Already applied in seeded GFC '08 (Q4 '08 ≈ -8% advance print).

q3_sloos_quarter_alignment.rule: For SLOOS C&I tightening, pin to the quarterly survey whose period contains the scenario peak window.
specifics: GFC '08 peak Oct '08 → SLOOS Q4 '08 (released Nov '08).
Document chosen quarter explicitly in source_note.
Already applied in seeded GFC '08 + COVID '20.

q4_pre_2013_rrp.rule: Drop the Reverse Repo balance entirely from pre-2013 scenarios.
specifics: RRP facility launched September 2013; pre-2013 scenarios cannot have a meaningful value.
Set value=null and as_of=null with explanatory source_note.
GFC '08 seed must be re-seeded — currently has rrp=0 which mechanically scores as a stress signal and distorts funding mechanism upward.
Funding mechanism for pre-2013 scenarios aggregates over 3 inputs (CPFF, STLFSI, bank reserves) instead of 4.

q5_aggregation_when_indicators_missing.rule: Re-percentile available indicators against the live sample, then take the simple-average of the AVAILABLE indicators in each mechanism panel. Composite is the simple-average of mechanism scores.
specifics: Same logic as the live cycle-mechanism scorer in scripts/compute_v11_mechanisms.py.
If a mechanism's panel has 4 inputs and a scenario has only 3 calibrated, mechanism score = simple-average of the 3.
No bespoke per-scenario aggregation rules. No imputation from correlated proxies.
Backtest tolerance bands (±15pp per mechanism, ±10 on composite) apply uniformly across scenarios regardless of how many indicators were calibrated.

indicators.source_note: Shiller CAPE bottomed near 13 Oct '87 from pre-crash ~16 (multpl.com / Shiller dataset)

v11 methodology: (1/CAPE × 100) − DGS10 monthly mean. 100/13.0 − 9.52% = -1.83%. Original calibration used a (1/CAPE − 10y TIPS) convention — retuned 2026-05-09 to match the methodology-v11.md formula and the indicator_history.json ERP series produced by fetch_history.py line 796 (ey monthly minus DGS10 monthly mean).
Wilshire 5000 Q4 '87 ≈ $2.5T / GDP ≈ $4.8T = ~52% (pre-crash Aug '87 ratio was ~71% — record at that time)
Per Q1 substitute: Moody's BAA corporate yield Oct '87 ≈ 11.5% minus 10y Treasury ~9.0% = ~250 bps. Source: FRED BAA + DGS10. BAMLH0A0HYM2 series begins 1996. Tag: proxy.

Per Q7 Senior Quant decision 2026-05-08 (extends Q1 substitute logic to IG): Moody's AAA corporate yield Oct '87 ≈ 10.5% minus 10y Treasury ~9.0% = ~150 bps. Source: FRED AAA + DGS10. BAMLC0A0CM series begins 1996. Tag: proxy.
Post-crash 3M CP-OIS spread spiked. Estimate ~80 bps Oct '87. DRAFT — verify against FRED CPF3M minus FFTAR archive (CPF3M series may have limited pre-2001 history).
WRESBAL ~$40B Oct '87 (pre-IOER era structural low — banks held minimal reserves; Fed didn't pay interest on reserves until 2008)
Oct 1987 ISM Manufacturing PMI = 60.4. Economy was strong; the crash was a financial-system shock, not real-economy stress.
Chicago Fed NFCI spiked to ~+1.0 Oct '87 (post-crash financial stress reading; FRED NFCI archive — series goes back to 1971 weekly)
WALCL ~+8% YoY Oct '87 (pre-balance-sheet-as-policy-tool era; modest growth phase)
M2 YoY ~+3% Oct '87 (declining from early-'80s levels)

Per Q1 substitute: VXO (Black-Scholes-derived precursor; CBOE constructed retroactively from S&P 100 options) spiked to ~150 on Oct 19 '87 close. DRAFT — VXO Oct '87 reconstructions vary by source (range 100–150); 150 represents the panic-peak reading. Tag: proxy.

60d correlation derived from S&P + Moody's BAA-Treasury proxy (BAMLH0A0HYM2 doesn't exist pre-1996). Estimate ~0.70 in Oct '87 panic. DRAFT — needs reconstruction in Phase 2C producer using BAA proxy.

indicators_dropped.reason: no historical sample available in indicator_history.json
Dropped — FRED STLFSI4 series begins December 1993. Funding mechanism re-aggregates per Q5 over CPFF + bank reserves (RRP also dropped per Q4).
Dropped — RRP facility launched Sep 2013 (post-scenario). Per Q4 methodology decision 2026-05-08.
Dropped — ISM Non-Manufacturing (Services) PMI series began 1997. Growth mechanism re-aggregates per Q5 over ISM Mfg + GDPNow proxy.
insufficient history (0 points post-2011-01-01); minimum 24 required
Dropped — SLOOS survey began 1990. Liquidity & policy mechanism re-aggregates per Q5 over ANFCI + Fed BS YoY + M2 YoY.
Dropped — CBOE SKEW Index began 1990. Positioning & breadth mechanism re-aggregates per Q5 over VIX (substitute VXO) + eq-credit correlation.
Dropped — MOVE Index began 1988. Positioning & breadth mechanism re-aggregates per Q5 (already noted under SKEW drop).

indicators.source_note: Shiller CAPE peaked 44.2 Mar '00 (record at time; per Shiller dataset)

v11 methodology: (1/CAPE × 100) − DGS10 monthly mean. 100/44.2 − 6.26% = -3.99%. Original calibration used a (1/CAPE − 10y TIPS) convention — retuned 2026-05-09 to match the methodology-v11.md formula and the indicator_history.json ERP series produced by fetch_history.py line 796 (ey monthly minus DGS10 monthly mean).
Wilshire 5000 / GDP ≈ 146% Mar '00 (record at time per Buffett's 2001 Fortune article)
FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2 ~470 bps Mar '00 (elevated late-cycle credit fears)
FRED BAMLC0A0CM ~135 bps Mar '00
3M CP-OIS spread ~30 bps Mar '00 (calm)
FRED STLFSI4 ~0 Mar '00 (calm — peak euphoria)
WRESBAL ~$40B Mar '00 (pre-IOER era structural low)
Mar 2000 ISM Manufacturing = 55.7 (still expanding)
Mar 2000 ISM Services = 58.2 (strong expansion)
FRED NFCI ~-0.5 Mar '00 (loose financial conditions)
WALCL ~+2% YoY Mar '00 (Y2K liquidity injection unwinding)
Q1 2000 SLOOS net % tightening C&I = +17 (modest late-cycle tightening; per Q3 pin to quarter containing the peak)
M2 YoY ~+5.5% Mar '00
CBOE SKEW ~115 Mar '00 (elevated tail-hedge demand even during peak euphoria)
VIX ~24 Mar 10 '00 Nasdaq peak (low for that era; pre-2008 VIX averaged higher than post-GFC norms)
Equity-credit 60d corr ~0.40 Mar '00 (low — bull-market complacency, equity and credit decoupled)
MOVE Index ~115 Mar '00 (rate-cycle vol from Fed tightening)
